entry.title: Re: Review Order Progress - Multilevel View
entry.content:
Good day @Moon3 

The behaviour you described is usually caused by planning data differences between systems (tenants) not by the app itself.

The PR to PO relation in Review Order Progress- Multilevel View is based on supply and demand elements. If one system (tenant) updates only after the next MRP run, it often means the planning situation is only recalculated during MRP in that tenant. The app itself was introduced and enhanced in recent releases but it does not replace the initial planning logic

The following blogs provide some good insights: DEMOs: App Review Order Progress - Multilevel View and

Manufacturing in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

You can compare both tenant environments by checking your MRP relevant master data, MRP run setup and execution timing, Procurement proposal conversion behaviour and your App filters and object selection consistency. This should show you why customising tenant shows immediate PR to PO change, while your test tenant requires another MRP initiation.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Programmatically retrieving lineage/source object info for Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to understand whether it is possible to programmatically retrieve lineage metadata for an Analytical Model in SAP Datasphere.

Use case:
Within the Datasphere UI, the Impact & Lineage view allows us to trace a field from an Analytical Model back to its underlying objects (views and source tables). I am currently developing a small web application intended to List AMs automatically along with few technical details(for example: fields, and their originating tables/views).

Using the Consumption OData API, I am already able to extract the list of fields of an Analytical Model:

GET https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/datasphere/consumption/analytical/<space_id>/<asset_id>/$metadata

This provides the semantic fields exposed by the model.


However, my requirement is to enrich this information with the technical source information, specifically:

Analytical Model Field → Datasphere View Column → Underlying Table (or Remote Table)

Example:

I would like to know:

  1. Is there a REST API available to retrieve lineage information similar to what is shown in the Datasphere Impact & Lineage diagram?

  2. If not, are there any supported system metadata tables/views (for example via SQL access to the space) that can be used to obtain this mapping?

Any guidance or recommended approach would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

SAP Datasphere API

entry.title: Re: how to reverse the client copy
entry.content:
Hello,

client copy deletes in first place all data in target client. So if you want to bring the data back, you probably have to do a restore for your system. there is no turning back feature in client copy. another thing would be that you do a client export of your target client in first place, so you can use this client export for restore and do not need to restore whole system.

also you can check out the copy profiles

https://help.sap.com/saphelp_snc700_ehp04/helpdata/en/4d/806794095114afe10000000a42189c/content.htm?no_cache=true

 

 

br

entry.title: Re: SmartForms: One Delivery Order with Multiple Customer...
entry.content:
Yes, what you’re seeing is standard SAP behavior. In SD billing, invoice splitting is controlled by split criteria. Even if the delivery is the same, SAP checks certain header fields when creating the billing document. One of those fields is the Customer PO number (VBKD-BSTKD). If items in the delivery reference different PO numbers, SAP treats them as requiring separate billing documents.
Check VTFL where you have two fields "Assignment" and "Reference" where it should not be "A" in which case, you will get invoice split. Also, you might be aware, the cause of the billing split is due to VBRK-ZUKRI. Check what value is there. Finally, implement custom validation to restrict this in such a way to generate one billing document against one delivery.  Once this is acheived, your Smartform issue will also be taken care automatically.

entry.title: Re: Secure Login Service Initial Authentication - Move aw...
entry.content:
Hi Kal,

 what you describe (Kerberos/SPNEGO → “virtual user” on Secure Login Server → short-lived X.509 → SNC SSO to ABAP) is exactly the kind of scenario that SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI is meant to solve when you want to move away from on-prem AD/KDC and still keep certificate-based SNC SSO for SAP GUI. 

Key point: With Secure Login Service for SAP GUI, the initial authentication can be done via your central IdP (e.g., Microsoft Entra ID) using modern flows (SAML-based browser login), and then the Secure Login Client obtains a short-lived X.509 certificate to do SNC SSO to your ABAP systems. No Kerberos/KDC dependency required. 

Does SAML fit here (instead of Kerberos)?

 SAML (or OIDC) fits as the initial authentication step (IdP login), and the resulting outcome is still an X.509 cert used for SNC to SAP GUI systems. This is exactly how SAP positions it for “pure Entra / no on-prem KDC” situations. 

About the “virtual user” concept

In many modern Secure Login Service deployments, you no longer need the same “virtual user on AS Java Secure Login Server” construct, because the server-side enrollment is provided as a cloud service (no AS Java dependency) and the identity is driven by the IdP authentication + mapping. 

SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI:
https://www.sap.com/germany/products/financial-management/secure-login-service-for-gui.html (SAP)

Regards Colt

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP - Profit Center Description Not Appearing in...
entry.content: Follow‑on Update Follow‑on update on Profit Center Description not visible in Location‑Product MDT As a follow‑up to my earlier post, I ran Adapt Planning Objects for the last 30 days, aligned with the period during which the configuration changes were made. Despite this, the Profit Center Description is still not visible in the Location‑Product (compound) master data type / planning object view. What has already been validated: The Profit Center Description attribute exists and is active in the Profit Center MDT. The attribute has been added to the Location‑Product MDT and is active. Relevant Planning Area checks are clean, and Adapt Planning Objects completed successfully for the impacted timeframe. The expectation is that Profit Center Description should be derived via join/lookup from Profit Center MDT based on Profit Center code, not manually maintained at Location‑Product level. In some planning templates, the value appears for limited combinations only, suggesting a possible propagation or dependent table update issue, rather than a pure UI problem. Excel add‑in cache effects were ruled out by fully closing and reopening Excel sessions (not just refresh). Current gap: Even after Adapt Planning Objects execution, the Profit Center Description does not consistently appear in Location‑Product MDT or downstream planning views for all valid combinations. Looking for expert guidance on further checks, specifically: Whether additional steps are required beyond Adapt Planning Objects to propagate referenced MDT attributes to compound MDTs / lower planning objects. Any known prerequisites or constraints around exposing referenced attributes (like descriptions) in compound MDTs. Whether Planning Level or PA‑attribute exposure can block visibility even when MDT configuration is correct. If this behavior could indicate a known limitation or defect in IBP master data propagation. Any insights or recommended diagnostics would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Unable to save prompt templates in SAP AI Launchpad
entry.content: Seems like it is mandatory to have the "ailaunchpad_genai_manager" role collection, or the role genai_manager to be able to save prompts. The roles prompt_manager, prompt_ecperimenter and genai_experimenter are not sufficient to be able to save the templates created. This only allows you to save the prompt as a whole

entry.title: Emarsys Forms questions
entry.content:
Hi,

I have some questions about Forms in Emarsys. Are the following things possible with them? 

1. Multi-lingual forms

I have noticed when you create a Form, that you need to choose 1 language. However, if you later add this Form into a Web Channel Campaign (Embedded or Overlay) and use a Content Block that allows to include a Form, I noticed it is possible to overwrite the labels that you have on the form. How far could you go with that? Could there somehow be variable labels added here that have a different value depending on the language of the website that this web channel campaign is added to?

2. Conditional fields

I have a single select dropdown field for "Type of Business". One of the options in this field is "Other". If the "Other" value is chosen, I would like an extra freetext field to appear where they can describe themselves their Other value. 

Or another example is if in the "Type of Business" list, the value "Restaurant" is chosen, that an extra field appears "how many seating spots do you have?".

Is this posible?

3. Hidden fields

Can you have hidden fields with a fixed value on a Form? For example the field "Country", that you could set to a fixed value "Belgium" in the background in a hidden field in the form, because this form would be deployed on a Belgian website.

Or, for a mini-form (with only an email address field) specifically to Subscribe to the newsletter, to have the Opt-in checkbox also selected & hidden on the background, as the form already specifies that it is specifically to subscribe.

4. Dynamic redirect url

You have a setting to which url the form needs to redirect after all the fields were correctly filled in and the form was submitted. This could be the url to a published brochure or leaflet. Do we need to create a separate form for each of our different brochures in each different language, or is there some way in which the redirect can be dynamic? i.e. the Website will have a page "download our Brochure A" where the form will be embedded, can it somehow then tell the form it should redirect to the download link for Brochure A, while on the website page to download Brochure B it can tell the same form to redirect to the download link for Brochure B?

entry.title: Re: yaai & yaai_ui – Open-Source AI Tools for ABAP Developers
entry.content:
Hi @Karthik-K26 ,
Thank you so much for your kind words and for taking the time to share your feedback. I’m really glad to hear that the blog was helpful and that everything worked well for you.

Regarding your question, this behavior is intentional. The function-calling output is meant to be consumed directly by the LLM, along with the previous messages and tool call history. Since the LLM processes it as part of the conversation context, it only needs a textual payload, which can be plain text, CSV, JSON, XML, or basically any format you want.

entry.title: Re: Display the attachedment directly in the web browser
entry.content:
Just copying the answer:

Yes, it’s possible, but it depends on the file type and server settings. Files like PDFs or images can open directly in the browser if the server sends them with the header Content-Disposition: inline instead of attachment. If your PR system enforces downloads for security, you’ll need to check with the admin or vendor to see if inline display is supported.

entry.title: Re: advanced variant configuration ETO public cloud
entry.content:
The issue isn’t with the code or URL, it’s with your data setup. The ETO profile is stricter than CTO — it requires a BOM view ID. Compare your custom BOM setup against the delivered scope item BOM and you’ll see the missing piece.

Next step

  • Assign a valid BOM view ID to your custom BOM.
  • If you only created a single-level BOM without specifying a view, add the “Production” or “Engineering” view.
  • Re-test the ETO profile. The error should disappear once the BOM view ID is available for binding.

entry.title: On the RPT-1 podcast: practical AI for SAP data (demos, lessons, and what’s next) 🎙️
entry.content:
If you work in SAP, you already know the truth: our world is rows, columns, master data, transactional history, and process context — not creative writing, in other words - SAP is not a “write me a poem” platform. That’s why I really enjoyed this conversation on SAP RPT-1, where we went beyond “AI hype” and into what’s genuinely useful for SAP practitioners.

RPT-1 is built for structured enterprise data. In plain terms: it’s designed to reason over the kinds of datasets we live in every day—think finance, supply chain, procurement, operations—where accuracy, traceability, and consistency matter more than fancy wording.

What you’ll get from the episode

  • A practical view of “AI for SAP data” — where it fits (and where it doesn’t)

  • Real demos and prototypes focused on outcomes, not theory

  • How to think about predictive + analytical use cases that start small but scale (without turning into a 6-month science project)

  • A clear message: move from chatbot experiments to measurable business impact

Why this matters for the SAP community

Most AI discussions ignore a key point: SAP value is locked in structured data + process discipline. The moment you align AI with that reality—tabular signals, business semantics, governance—you shift from “cool demo” to real operational leverage.

If you’re exploring AI for SAP and you want something that’s grounded in enterprise reality (data quality, governance, repeatability, outcomes), this episode is a solid watch:

🎥Watch here my podcast with Holger and Goran from Microsoft:
https://youtu.be/CbUDRgEO0yI?si=J4vE-BZ0Xig_srYo



Please share your thoughts and feedback! 

Cheers,
Amit Lal

entry.title: Prevent negative balance in Investment WBS – SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear community,

My objective is to prevent investment projects (WBS elements) from ending up with a negative balance in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

I have the following issue:
• PO assigned to an investment WBS
• Goods Receipt posted → cost hits the WBS
• Settlement to AuC executed
• Afterwards, the GR is reversed

With event-based investment profiles, there is no issue because the GR reversal also reverses the settlement to the AuC automatically.

However, with non-event-based settlement, the settlement is not reversed automatically and the WBS remains with a negative balance.

Is there any way to solve this?
Could you please share ideas on how to prevent the WBS from ending up with a negative balance in this scenario?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP EWM License Audit Tools
entry.content:
SAP EWM License Audit Tools

Introduction

Managing SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) licenses across complex enterprise landscapes has always been challenging, particularly when dealing with decentralized warehouse operations. Organizations often struggle to maintain clear visibility into their license compliance across distributed EWM environments, leading to potential audit risks and unexpected costs.

SAP has now enhanced the self-auditing report /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_SELF_AUDIT to provide better transparency for decentralized EWM systems. This enhancement addresses a critical gap in license tracking for decentralized environments, ensuring organizations can confidently monitor their EWM usage patterns and maintain compliance with their licensing agreements.

The enhancement eliminates guesswork in license management and provides the clarity needed for confident compliance monitoring across both centralized and decentralized warehouse operations.

What's New: Enhanced License Self-Auditing

The enhanced /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_SELF_AUDIT report now provides specialized handling for decentralized EWM systems with a key improvement: clear identification through a 'DECNTRAL' identifier in the 'Actual Usage' field. This enhancement transforms how organizations track and manage licenses across their EWM landscape.

You can use this report to select and review a list of tracked warehouse numbers based on their usage of basic or advanced functions, monitoring compliance throughout the last 12 months. The report provides comprehensive visibility into delivery items per working day, total delivery items per warehouse, and compliance status across your entire EWM environment,

The benefits extend beyond simple identification – organizations now have unified visibility into license compliance across both centralized and decentralized warehouse operations, streamlining audit processes and reducing compliance risks.

Understanding Decentralized EWM Systems

Key Characteristics

Decentralized EWM represents a deployment model where Extended Warehouse Management runs on a separate system from SAP S/4HANA while maintaining full integration with the core solution for master data and transactional data, this architecture provides enhanced performance levels and ensures warehouse business process continuity during system maintenance or downtime.

Unlike embedded EWM, which shares the same client as SAP S/4HANA, decentralized EWM operates independently while seamlessly communicating with the enterprise management system for critical business processes. This separation allows for dedicated warehouse processing power and improved system resilience.

License Classification

A fundamental principle of decentralized EWM licensing is that these systems are always considered Advanced Warehouse Management. This classification is automatic and non-negotiable – there is no "basic" license option for decentralized deployments.

This Advanced classification becomes transparent in the 'Acknowledged Usage of Warehouse Management' field, ensuring complete visibility into the licensing implications of choosing a decentralized architecture. Organizations planning decentralized EWM implementations must budget for Advanced licenses from the outset, as the license type differentiation for Basic or Advanced is only applicable for embedded deployment cases,

How to Use Enhanced Report

Step-by-Step Process

  1. Navigate to Transaction: Access /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_SELF_AUDIT through transaction SE38 or directly via the transaction code

  1. Configure Selection Parameters: Enter specific warehouse numbers or client details, or leave blank to review all warehouses in the system
  2. Execute the Report: Run the analysis to generate comprehensive usage and compliance data
  3. Review Results: Examine the output for compliance status, usage patterns, and system identification

Reading the Results

The enhanced report now clearly displays 'DECNTRAL' in the 'Actual Usage' field for decentralized systems. This immediate identification helps distinguish system types during audit reviews. The report provides monthly breakdowns of Basic and Advanced feature usage, including total delivery items per month, average items per working day, and compliance status from both usage and customization perspectives,

For decentralized systems, the 'Acknowledged Usage of Warehouse Management' field will consistently show Advanced Warehouse Management, reflecting the mandatory license classification. The report tracks compliance over the last 12 months, providing historical visibility for trend analysis and audit preparation.

Important Limitations to Know

Supported Reports on Embedded Systems

While the self-auditing report has been enhanced, two other license-related reports remain unsupported in decentralized EWM environments: /SCWM/RP_COMPLIANCE_CHECK and /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_ANALYSIS, attempting to execute these reports in decentralized systems will generate error messages.

The good news is that SAP has improved these error messages to make the limitation transparent, reducing confusion for users who might attempt to run unsupported reports. The error message "/SCWM/TM033" now clearly indicates that the transaction or program is not supported for decentralized systems.

For Embedded System use the report /SCWM/RP_COMPLIANCE_CHECK and /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_ANALYSIS

 

Report /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_ANALYSIS 

Best Practice: Stick to Self-Auditing

For decentralized systems, /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_SELF_AUDIT is the only supported license auditing report. This consolidation simplifies the compliance process by providing a single, comprehensive tool for license management in decentralized environments.

Organizations should establish processes that rely exclusively on the self-auditing report for decentralized EWM license compliance, avoiding attempts to use the unsupported alternatives. This approach ensures consistent and reliable license tracking across the entire EWM landscape.

Business Impact and Benefits

For License Managers

Enhanced reporting provides unprecedented visibility into decentralized system usage patterns, enabling more accurate license planning and compliance monitoring. License managers can now easily identify which systems are decentralized and understand their Advanced license requirements briefly.

The 12-month historical view helps with trend analysis and future license planning, while the clear compliance indicators reduce audit preparation time and effort. This transparency eliminates the risk of unexpected license compliance issues during audits.

For IT Teams

Technical teams benefit from clearer error messaging when attempting to use unsupported reports, reducing troubleshooting time and confusion. The enhanced self-auditing report provides all necessary technical details for system administration and compliance verification.

The standardization on a single reporting tool for decentralized systems simplifies training requirements and reduces the complexity of license management procedures. IT teams can focus on maintaining compliance through one reliable reporting mechanism.

Implementation Considerations

Organizations using decentralized EWM should ensure they have the latest version of the self-auditing report, which may require implementing SAP Note 3712523. The enhancement is particularly relevant for organizations running decentralized EWM on S/4HANA or classic EWM systems.

Integration with existing license management processes should account for the automatic Advanced license classification of decentralized systems. Training programs should emphasize the limitations of other license reports in decentralized environments and focus on the enhanced self-auditing capabilities.

Regular execution of the self-auditing report should become part of standard compliance procedures, with particular attention to the new 'DECNTRAL' identifiers and their implications for license management. Organizations should establish monthly or quarterly review cycles to maintain optimal compliance visibility.

Conclusion

The enhanced EWM license self-auditing functionality represents a significant step forward in managing license compliance for decentralized warehouse operations. By providing clear identification of decentralized systems and their Advanced license requirements, organizations can maintain better control over their EWM licensing landscape.

For organizations operating or planning decentralized EWM implementations, this enhancement eliminates ambiguity and provides the transparency needed for confident license management. The consolidation of license auditing into a single, comprehensive report simplifies compliance processes while ensuring complete visibility into system usage patterns.

We recommend that all organizations with decentralized EWM systems implement this enhancement and establish regular self-auditing procedures to maintain optimal license compliance and avoid potential retrospective penalties.

Additional Resources

  • SAP Note 3712523: Enhanced Self Auditing Report for decentralized systems
  • SAP Note 3392135: Self Audit Report implementation
  • SAP Note 3699217: Error handling for unsupported reports in decentralized systems
  • Transaction /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_SELF_AUDIT: Self-auditing report execution
  • SAP Help Portal: Extended Warehouse Management documentation

Hope that can help you.

Thank you and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Pubic Cloud assigning the same accounting principle to two ledgers
entry.content:
When using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud with a two ledger scenario (0L, 2L), is it possible to assign the same accounting principle ATAP as group accounting principle to ledger 0L and as local accounting principle to the company code in the local ledger 2L?

According to the SAP help article "Assign Accounting Principles to Ledgers and Company Codes" it is possible. We want to gain an official SAP statement. 

"You can assign the same accounting principle to more than one standard ledger only if the leading ledger is one of these standard ledgers. This condition must be met for every company code."

Best regards, Boris

entry.title: Welcome to our Supply Chain Management Learning Group!
entry.content:
WELCOME!

We are very excited to launch this brand-new learning group for all learners of SAP solutions in the Supply Chain space.

This is a space where learning, collaboration, and real-world supply chain experience come together — a place where ideas grow, questions are welcome, and learning becomes a shared journey rather than a solo effort. Whether you ask questions, share insights, or simply follow along and learn at your own pace, you are part of this community.

Here you can connect with SAP experts and fellow learners across all SAP Supply Chain topics and be among the first to hear about new courses, live sessions, and certification updates.

The focus of this community is learning. It complements the existing SAP product communities, which remain the right place for detailed product discussions and system troubleshooting. Here, the emphasis is on understanding concepts, developing skills, and progressing along your SAP Supply Chain learning journey.

This is a safe space to discuss SAP learning content such as courses, learning journeys, hands-on exercises, and certifications. No question is too basic — we are all learners here. Questions related to learning systems and training exercises are very welcome.

The community is moderated by SAP experts who are involved in creating and delivering official learning content and live sessions, so you are connecting directly with the team behind the training.

The solutions that we plan to cover in this community include:

  • Production Planning
  • Manufacturing & Digital Manufacturing
  • Project Systems & Portfolio and Project Management
  • Extended Warehouse Management
  • Variant Configuration
  • Product Engineering

Before you leave, make sure to click Join Group so you can ask questions, participate in discussions, and share your thoughts. Also remember to subscribe to the learning community so you don’t miss any updates.

 

SAP Community provides a separate place for discussions and questions, but we thought we’d try and simply things for you by combining them. After all, a question often leads to a discussion and vice-versa. So please use the Discussions button to start a discussion and to ask questions.

 

Why not introduce yourself to members of this community and let us know a little about your SAP journey? What brought you here? What are you hoping to get from this community? Simply post a reply here and let us know.

Make sure you spread the word about this community. Post a link in your social media so that we can build a vibrant and effective community of data and analytics learners.

As with all SAP communities, we want this to be a safe space for our members. Please familiarize yourself with the SAP Community Rules of Engagement

Let’s connect, learn, and grow together –  we look forward to building this Supply Chain learning community with you.

entry.title: Meet Your Supply Chain Management Team
entry.content:
🌎Your SAP Supply Chain Management Expert Team👥

A dedicated team of experienced SAP Supply Chain experts supports this learning group, and we’re excited to introduce them to you:

Frank Layer

Hi SAP SCM Colleagues around the globe - enchanté! My name is Frank Layer and I'm working for the SAP Learning department in the role of a learning architect. From an outside perspective, I support our Portfolio Management in bridging the gap between educational needs and learning content delivery, with a strong focus on learning content management, development, and tools. In former times you might have seen me as an instructor for SAP Production Planning in classroom trainings, mostly in the German-speaking area. Looking forward to our collaboration in this learning group!

 

Steffen Jaeger

Welcome to the SCM Community!
My name is Steffen Jäger, and I’m the responsible coordinator for Manufacturing within our SCM topic areas. Additionally, I serve as a subject matter expert in Warehouse Management, EPPM, and Manufacturing. I'm happy to support the community by sharing expertise, guiding discussions, and helping connect the dots across our end‑to‑end processes. In SAP Learning, I’m responsible for developing and delivering training content, besides coordinating our overall Manufacturing course portfolio.
My passion is enabling others—whether through structured learning or everyday knowledge exchange—so that we can collectively strengthen our capabilities and move our SCM topics forward. I’m excited to welcome you to this community and look forward to learning, sharing, and growing together.
Feel free to reach out, start discussions, and bring in your ideas!

Stefan Luetkehoff

Hello SAP SCM colleagues worldwide, 
Welcome to our Supply Chain Management Learning Group! My name is Stefan Lütkehoff, and I am part of the SAP Learning department, where I support the development of learning content. You may also recognize me from classroom trainings on SAP R&D Engineering, particularly in the German-speaking regions. I look forward to collaborating with all of you in this learning group.

Andreas Koelbl

After graduating as a mechanical engineer (Diplom-Ingenieur) in 1997, I worked for several companies as an SAP consultant. My focus was on production planning and control as well as work preparation processes. In this role, I supported a wide range of national and international clients from various industries, with mechanical engineering always being my main area of expertise. In 2014, I joined SAP Deutschland SE & Co. KG as a Solution Architect in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) division. Since 2016, I have been part of the software development team at SAP SE in Walldorf, initially working as Product Owner for the SAP S/4HANA for Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) engine. In 2020, I moved into Product Management and at the same time became the responsible Area Product Owner for AVC. In 2025, I transitioned into my new role as a Subject Matter Expert for classification and variant configuration in the SAP Learning department. I am co-author of the book Advanced Variant Configuration with SAP S/4HANA (German: ISBN 978-3-8362-9038-8; English: ISBN 978-1-4932-2359-6). Outside of work, and besides spending time with my three kids, I am passionate about bouldering and mountaineering.

entry.title: Upcoming: SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 (Virtual Event)
entry.content:
Let’s Spread the Word: SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 

From February 24–26, 2026, SAP will host the SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days as a three-day virtual event. The event will showcase the latest innovations and solution highlights across the SAP PLM portfolio, with a strong focus on collaboration, efficiency, and digital transformation.

Agenda overview:

  • Day 1 – Feb 24, 2026: PLM solutions for discrete industries

  • Day 2 – Feb 25, 2026: PLM solutions for process industries

  • Day 3 – Feb 26, 2026: Project & Portfolio Management

Participants will also have the opportunity to connect directly with SAP experts and gain insights into current developments and best practices.

The virtual format also allows for flexible participation — you can join for all three days or attend selected sessions that best fit your interests and schedule.

If you are interested, you can register for the event here. 

This is a valuable opportunity to expand your knowledge and engage with SAP experts. We look forward to your participation!

entry.title: New Certification Available: SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Production
entry.content:
New Certification Available: SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Production

We are excited to announce that the new certification SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Production Planning and Manufacturing is now live.

This certification is designed for consultants working in the SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing environment and validates core skills required to actively contribute to implementation projects. It applies to both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA on-premise solutions.

What This Certification Confirms

The certification verifies that candidates have a solid understanding of:

  • Manufacturing processes in SAP S/4HANA

  • Production Planning

  • Production execution and shop floor processes

  • Material components and master data

  • Capacity planning

  • Advanced Planning with PP/DS

It demonstrates both business process knowledge and technical capabilities relevant for real-world project environments.

Recommended Learning Journey

To prepare for the certification, SAP offers the learning journey:

Implementing Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

This comprehensive program covers:

  • Core manufacturing business processes

  • Production Planning fundamentals

  • Advanced Planning with PP/DS

  • Shopfloor Control for discrete and process industries

  • From master data setup to production order execution

  • Capacity planning and optimization

The learning journey provides structured guidance and hands-on knowledge to ensure participants are fully prepared for the certification exam.

Staying Certified

SAP certifications are valid for 12 months.
To keep your certification active, you can extend it by successfully completing the required assessment before expiration. SAP provides personalized notifications to help you stay up to date.

Continuous learning and regular certification updates ensure that your skills remain aligned with the latest SAP innovations.

Get Started

You can find detailed information about the certification here:

Please help

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud: How to create SmartEdit editor for a new relation in CMS Component
entry.content:
Introduction

SmartEdit is often enough "smart" to deduce how new CMS component can be edited. However when complex attribute is declared as new relation between CMS Component and composed type, SmartEdit needs to be customised to edit this field. 

We have official documentation that explains how simple custom attribute can be edited : Customizing the Generic Editor to Support New Field Types (Optional)

This article completes this documentation for the relation/collection and it gives an overview how that can be achieved. 

To start this guideline, let imagine a scenario where we want to display a list of vouchers attached to banner CMS component.

 

Data Model

First we need to declare this new relation in SAP Commerce data model

*items.xml

<relation code="Banner2VouchersRelation" autocreate="true"
   generate="true" localized="false">
   <sourceElement type="SimpleResponsiveBannerComponent" 
qualifier="bannerVouchers"
    cardinality="one">
   <modifiers read="true" write="true" search="true" optional="true" />
    </sourceElement>
    <targetElement type="Voucher" qualifier="vouchers"
      collectiontype="list" cardinality="many" ordered="true">
   <modifiers read="true" write="true" search="true" optional="true" partof="false" />
   </targetElement>
</relation>

 

CMS API

By default a new relation is mapped as EditableDropdown. This mapping is transmitted through cmsStructureType.  

First step is to create a new mapping to ensure that new type can be captured by SmartEdit. According to our scenario, we can called it: MultiVoucherSelector and SingleVoucherSelector

Let create a new beans that inject this new cmsStructureType values for the collection and for the item into the collection.

cmsfacades-structuretypes-populators-spring.xml

<bean id="multiVoucherSelectorPopulator" 
class="de.hybris.platform.cmsfacades.types.populator.CmsStructureTypeComponentTypeAttributePopulator">
 <property name="cmsStructureType" value="MultiVoucherSelector" />
</bean>

<bean id="voucherTypeAttributePopulator" class="de.hybris.platform.cmsfacades.types.populator.CmsStructureTypeComponentTypeAttributePopulator">
 <property name="cmsStructureType" value="SingleVoucherSelector" />
</bean>

Then, we need to create a new bean for data model type mapping

cmsfacades-predicate-spring.xml

<bean id="isVoucherAssignableFromPredicate" 
parent="cmsAssignableFromAttributePredicate" 
p:typeCode="Voucher"/>

And finally, we need to create beans for collection mapping for this type

cmsfacades-structuretypes-generic-config-spring.xml

<entry>
  <key>
   <ref bean="isVoucherAssignableFromPredicate" />
  </key>
  <list>
   <ref bean="voucherTypeAttributePopulator" />
  </list>
</entry>

<entry>
 <key>
 <bean class="de.hybris.platform.cmsfacades.types.service.predicate.MultiCatalogAwareItemAttributePredicate">
 <property name="isCollectionPredicate" ref="isCollectionPredicate" />
  <property name="assignableFromAttributePredicate"
   ref="isVoucherAssignableFromPredicate" />
 </bean>
</key>
 <list>
  <ref bean="multiVoucherSelectorPopulator" />
 </list>
</entry>

 

SmartEdit Container

In SmartEdit side, we can declare field mapping for both new cmsStructureType types. For each new type, we need to create dedicated Angular component that will manage user interface.

For the creation of this new components, you can take inspiration from existing editor MultiProductSelectorComponent

smartedittrailcontainerModule.ts

providers: [
...
moduleUtils.initialize(
 (editorFieldMappingService: EditorFieldMappingService) => {
   editorFieldMappingService.addFieldMapping(
     'MultiVoucherSelector',
       null,
       null,
      {
        component: MultiVoucherEditorComponent
      });
      },
      [EditorFieldMappingService]
),
moduleUtils.initialize(
  (editorFieldMappingService: EditorFieldMappingService) => {
     editorFieldMappingService.addFieldMapping(
      'SingleVoucherSelector',
       null,
       null,
       {
         component: SingleVoucherEditorComponent
       });
     },
     [EditorFieldMappingService]
 )
]

 

Troubleshoots

If you see this following message in console.log that means cmsStructureType is returning  EditableDropdown

log.service.ts:48 No dropdown populator found

And you can debug cmsStructureType value by adding breakpoint in EditorFieldMappingService

if you see this following message in console.log that means your field mapping was not loaded correctly from your custom SmartEdit container extension.

log.service.ts:48 editorFieldMappingService - 
Cannot find suitable field mapping for type MultiVoucherSelector

 

Tips

During your SmartEdit customisation is not necessary to recompile fully SAP Commerce between each change. You can do hot SmartEdit app recompilation and deployment just by executing this following commands

cd hybris/bin/modules/smartedit/smartedittools
ant rushupdatefull -Dpath=./
ant rushrebuilddev -Dpath=./

And to get details on the compilation error, please ensure that following property has been setup

npmancillary.rush.build.dev.mode=true

 

Conclusion

In this article, we saw how SmartEdit maps CMS component attributes and how can we can define a new custom mapping. Angular component creation is not detailed. This part should be straight forward and close to classic Angular component implementation.

However, if you're encountering some difficulty please contact SAP expert service team that should be able to help you : sapcx-expertservices@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Currency type 31 and currency type 11 are not getting...
entry.content:
Currency types 11 and 31 are “freely defined currencies” you’ve set up in FINSC_LEDGER. FAGL_FCV only updates currencies that are flagged as valuation-relevant in the ledger settings. By default, freely defined currencies are not automatically updated by valuation runs unless you explicitly configure them in the currency valuation settings. The valuation program reads the currency types from the ledger customizing (FINSC_LEDGER) and from the valuation area settings. If the freely defined currencies aren’t marked for valuation, they remain blank in the PRCHG document.

So the difference you’re seeing is not a bug, it is because freely defined currencies don’t automatically participate in FAGL_FCV postings unless explicitly configured.

entry.title: SAP on IBM i: Watch out for <sid>OFR user profiles
entry.content:
When SAP R/3 was first released for OS/400 in 1996, the designers wanted to create a look and feel for system administrators as close as possible to the familiar operating system OS/400. Part of that was to create user profiles like <sid>OFR for the main administrator or <sid>OPR for an operator with limited authority in analogy to QSECOFR and QSYSOPR in OS/400. Over time, it turned out that these special user profiles required a lot of adjustments in SAP installation and upgrade procedures, so it was decided to align the user profile concept with other operating system platforms and replace these user profiles with a user profile <sid>ADM. This was first introduced in 2008 with the release of SAP NetWeaver 7.1 and is documented in SAP Note 1123501.

Obviously, a lot of SAP systems existed at that time that were still using the old or “classic” user concept. With the availability of downward-compatible kernels, it was suggested to convert existing SAP systems to use the new user concept. To ease this conversion, a tool named CONVUSRCPT was created and documented in SAP Note 1149318. This tool creates all necessary user profiles and adjusts ownership and authorities, but it does not delete the old user profiles that are no longer needed. So, even if your system was running with the new user concept and user profile <sid>ADM, the old user profile <sid>OFR would still be around. If y system has been newly installed in SAP release 7.1 or higher, only user profile <sid>ADM exists, but no <sid>OFR.

In order to create the SAP user profiles during installations or upgrades in a consistent way with all required group profiles and authorities, SAP is shipping a tool named CRTSAPUSR in the kernel library (command and program object). Recently, a vulnerability was found and fixed with SAP Note 3627373 and its associated patches. Unfortunately, a new error was introduced to the CRTSAPUSR tool that resulted in situations where the job’s current user profile was changed to USRCLS(*PGMR) INLMNU(*SIGNOFF) LMTCPB(*YES) when CRTSAPUSR USER(*OFR) was called by the SAP initial program SAPINLPGM. The details of these errors are described in SAP Note 3701638, SAP Note 3711902 and some other places.

If you apply the ILE patches that are associated with SAP Note 3701638, the problem is solved, but you have to apply the patches in all parts of your system. Before an SAP upgrade, they must also be applied to the kernel directory of the upgrade. If that is not possible or not easy to perform, you can take a closer look at your <sid>OFR user profiles and delete them, if possible. These are the steps to do it:

  1. Find out if you still have user profiles named <sid>OFR on your system. You can use any tool of your choice. For example, the IBM Navigator for i has a feature to list all user profiles that contain the string “OFR”. If you do not find user profiles named <sid>OFR, everything is fine, and you are done.
  2. Find out if the user profiles <sid>OFR belong to SAP systems that are still running with the old (or “classic”) user concept. Any system prior to SAP NetWeaver 7.0 can only run with the old user concept, but those systems are also not affected by this bug as no patches were created for releases that were no longer in maintenance by SAP. As a precautionary action against vulnerability CVE-2025-42958, you should follow the instructions in SAP Note 3628734, section 1 for those systems. Otherwise check, if the work processes of your SAP system are running with user profile <sid>ADM (new user concept) or with user profiles <sid><nn>, <nn> being the SAP instance number (old user concept). If your SAP system is running with the old user concept and a supported, downward-compatible kernel (release 7.22 or higher), consider converting to the new user concept as documented in SAP Note 1149318. Afterwards, continue with the next step.
  3. If your SAP systems are running with the new user concept, the <sid>OFR user profiles are no longer needed. Before deleting them, find out if they own objects that are still needed. The command to show their owned objects is WRKOBJOWN USRPRF(<sid>OFR). Typically, they should own very few objects that are deleted when the user profile gets deleted or can easily be transferred to user profile <sid>ADM. If you see many objects being owned by <sid>OFR, check carefully if you still need these objects and who should own them when <sid>OFR gets deleted.
  4. Delete user profile <sid>OFR and transfer the remaining owned objects to <sid>ADM. The command for that is DLTUSRPRF USRPRF(<sid>OFR) OWNOBJOPT(*CHGOWN <sid>ADM) PGPOPT(*CHGPGP <sid>ADM). After that, you should no longer be affected by the reported problems.

entry.title: Claude Code + Python + SAP GUI Scripting: Your AI Agent for Any SAP Transaction
entry.content:
The Vision: Talk to your SAP system

Do you use still SAP transactions? Talk with your SAP system! AI can do the same things like you but faster and smarter!

What the agent does:

  • ✅Connect to your live SAP GUI session via COM
  • ✅Navigate through SAP transactions
  • ✅Export data and do analysis on it

🛠️ What We Built: The SAP GUI Scripting AI Agent

This Python application combines cutting-edge AI frameworks with battle-tested SAP automation:

Tech Stack:
├── 🧠 LangGraph (state machine workflows)
├── 🔗 LangChain (tool calling + LLM reasoning)  
├── 🖥️ pywin32 (COM automation for SAP GUI)
├── 📊 openpyxl (Excel generation)
├── 📚 sap_scripting.py (reusable SAP)
└── 📝 CLAUDE.md (living project documentation)

Core Capabilities Demonstrated

Capability Description

Live SAP GUI ConnectionROT-based COM access to running sessions
Intelligent NavigationTransaction execution, screen exploration, button discovery
Complex Grid ParsingTree controls, ALV grids, splitter shells, dynamic content
Error AnalysisMessage classification, severity scoring, root cause mapping
Excel AutomationStructured remediation plans with SAP transaction references
Conversational InterfaceNatural language → structured SAP operations

📊The Real-World Use Case: DMLT Posting Engine Error Analysis

Context: Migration project has 14,000+ posting simulation errors

Top 3 Critical Errors (from 10 total message classes)

# Message Count Severity Solution

1NR 751 - Number range interval missing9999+🔴HIGHFBN1/SNRO → Maintain number ranges in target
2CNV_PE 451 - TRule error2,328🔴HIGHCNV_PE_CUSTOM → Read Customizing + Regenerate TRules
3CNV_OT_CX 000 - Generic exception1,746🔴HIGHST22/SM21 → Check dumps/system log for root cause

Complete Error Analysis Generated

Message Count Severity Root Cause SAP Transaction

NR 7519999+HIGHNumber range gapFBN1/SNRO
CNV_PE 4512,328HIGHTRule customizationCNV_PE_CUSTOM
CNV_OT_CX 0001,746HIGHSystem exceptionST22/SM21
F5A 00286MEDIUMVendor deletion flagXK02/FK02
CNV_PE 58954MEDIUMWL2MOD exit errorDebug ABAP exit
CNV_PE 20551MEDIUMReference doc missingPost ref docs first
F5 35117MEDIUMAccount blockedFS00/FK02
F5A 00314MEDIUMG/L deletion flagFS00
F5 02610LOWMissing bank detailsFK02
/SLO/PECON 1025LOWWHT data missingFK02

🏗️ Architecture: How the Agent Works

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    User: Natural Language Request               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                 │
                                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. COM Connection → session.Info → System validation           │
│  2. /SLO/PECON → Error Monitor (ERR_OV) → F8 Execute           │
│  3. Tree Control parsing → MSGNO/MSGCNT_RCV/MSGTEXT extraction │
│  4. LLM Reasoning → Severity classification + solution mapping  │
│  5. openpyxl → Structured Excel with 2 sheets                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                 │
                                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Output: PE_Error_Analysis_ZFI_SDT.xlsx             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SAP Connection Pattern (ROT-based COM)

import win32com.client

# Connect to RUNNING SAP session (never launch from script)
SapGuiAuto = win32com.client.GetObject("SAPGUI")
application = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
connection = application.Children(0)   # first connection
session = connection.Children(0)       # first session

# Validate connection
print(f"System: {session.Info.SystemName}")
print(f"Client: {session.Info.Client}")
print(f"User: {session.Info.User}")

💰Why This Matters

Traditional Approach AI Agent Approach

Manual Error Monitor → Export → Excel → Manual analysis → 4+ hours"Check errors and create Excel" → ✅Done in 2 minutes
Static SQL queries → Miss dynamic tree dataLive GUI parsing → Always current
Generic error listsSeverity-ranked remediation with exact transactions
Consultant dependencySelf-service analysis for any team member

💡Immediate ROI: One agent run = 3-4 hours saved × €100/hour consultant rate = €300-400 value per execution

💬The Conversation That Built This

Here's the actual conversation flow with Claude Code that produced this solution:

User: "List SAP connections"
Agent: → Found 2 connections

User: "Test System"
Agent: → Connected to system

User: "Open /SLO/PECON"
Agent: → Transaction opened, PECON Start screen displayed

User: "Go into Error Monitor, check messages, create Excel with solutions"
Agent: → Clicked ERR_OV → F8 Execute → Parsed tree (16 nodes)
      → Generated PE_Error_Analysis.xlsx with 10 errors + solutions

Total time: ~2 minutes. Zero manual SAP navigation.

✅Prerequisites (Production-Ready)

  • ✅Windows OS + SAP GUI for Windows
  • ✅Scripting enabled (Alt+F12 → Options → Scripting)
  • ✅RZ11: sapgui/user_scripting = TRUE
  • ✅Open SAP session before agent execution
  • ✅Python 3.11+ with virtual environment
  • ✅Outlook (optional, for email automation)

📁Project Structure

sap_gui_scripting_ai/
├── 🐍 sap_gui_scripting_ai.py     # Main LangGraph agent
├── 📚 sap_scripting.py            # SAP COM library
├── 📝 CLAUDE.md                   # Living documentation (auto-updated)
├── 📊 PE_Error_Analysis.xlsx      # Generated output
└── 📁 posting_engine_knowledge/   # SAP docs + extracted knowledge
    ├── PECON_User_Guide_v2.1.pdf
    ├── PECON_FAQ_v1.5.pdf
    └── PE_Enablement_2025_10.pdf

 

❓FAQs

Q: Does this replace SAP GUI scripting consultants? A: It augments them. The agent handles 80% of repetitive analysis; consultants focus on exceptions.

Q: Is this safe for Production SAP? A: Read-only analysis mode. No postings, no data changes. Uses your existing session permissions.

Q: What SAP systems are supported? A: Any SAP GUI system with scripting enabled — ECC, S/4HANA, any client. Multi-system aware.

Q: Can it handle custom Z-transactions? A: Yes! Recursive screen exploration + visual debugging discovers any SAP screen pattern.

Q: What LLM powers the agent? A: Currently using Claude (Anthropic) via LangChain, but architecture supports OpenAI, Azure, or local models.

📣What's Coming in Part 1?

  • 🎥Live demo video (agent solving NR 751 in real-time)
  • 🛠️ Step-by-step setup (5 minutes to first execution)
  • 🔧Advanced patterns (Outlook integration, multi-session handling)
  • 📈Scaling to production (error handling, logging, monitoring)
  • 💻Full source code walkthrough

👍Like this post if you want Part 1 next week!

Share your SAP challenges in the comments below — which analysis blocks you most often?

entry.title: 5 good reasons to use Cloud ALM in all your SAP projects
entry.content:
Cloud ALM was released several years ago as a project & application life-cycle management tool. Put simply, it’s a tool designed to  help you run the tasks involved during project, as well as managing the applications after the project go live. In this very short blog, I will outline my arguments for why you should use it in your SAP projects, with a particular focus on SAP SuccessFactors.

Here are my top reasons why I recommend customers and partners use Cloud ALM in projects.

  • Easy to learn to use– the tool is relatively easy to learn how to use. There are numerous trainings available which support the user in the ramp up, but most of all there is a demo system that can be accessed to create a sample project and explore the features and navigation. By following the trainings and using the demo system you can ramp up quickly.
  • Customer Driven tool – the tool is designed to be used by the customer and partner together to manage the project, but the “location” of the tool sits with the customer. By this, I mean that its connected to a customer’s system landscape. So, all of their cloud systems can be made visible to them, and they have immediate and permanent access to the project information and resources. That means, if used correctly, that 2 years after a project is finished if questions arise, there is no need to contact their implementation partner for resources as the project plans and outcomes are visible in their Cloud ALM instance.
  • Immediate and up to date project status reporting. - If you use the tool to manage your project, you get an accurate status of the project phases and can report on them. This reduces the effort involved in checking with others if tasks are done or creating reporting separately. The tool has immediate reports that show the actual status of the phases, what is finished, what is left to do and what the blockers are. These can be shared with the stakeholders quickly and as often as needed.
  • Sticking to the plan -The tool is built on the activate methodology and includes all the tasks that need to be completed to run a successful project. So, it helps you to ensure that nothing is forgotten about or missing, or an incorrect or incomplete plan is used. The Quality Gate approach helps to ensure each phase progresses correctly and with oversight. Recently, Quality Gates have been added for SAP SuccessFactors There are various pre-built project templates that can be used for the different SAP Solutions, including SAP SuccessFactors. On top of this custom project templates can be uploaded if as a partner implementation team, you have your own best practice approach.
  • Required SAP Skills – Project management is always a desirable skill for anyone to have. If you combine having SAP Activate knowledge and experience with SAP Cloud ALM knowledge and experience, it increases your marketability as a professional be that on the customer, partner or SAP side.

Interested and want to get started?

The first place to start is the Cloud ALM Homepage. It includes all the links you will need to learn about the tool, get your hands on a demo, and request your own customer or partner instance. There are also regularly scheduled trainings and calls that take place throughout the year which can also be found on the page under the events schedule page. If you want a more detailed look at all of the features, I recommend searching the Application Help Guide.

 

Not convinced yet or have questions on the above? – Feel free to add a question or comment below and I will do my best to answer.

Paul T

entry.title: Re: Balancing field "Profit Center" in line item 000 not...
entry.content:
Balancing fields (like profit center, segment, business area) must be filled in every line item of a FI posting. In settlement, the system derives the profit center from the receiver object (your WBS element) and from the sender object (network activity). If there’s a mismatch or if one of the objects doesn’t pass a profit center, the system ends up with a blank profit center in one line, triggering the error. Common causes are:

  • Profit center not maintained consistently in the master data (network header, WBS element, settlement rule).
  • Profit center inheritance not active (e.g., WBS set but network activity not inheriting).
  • Account assignment object missing profit center derivation in OKB9 or substitution rules.
  • Settlement profile not set to derive profit center correctly.

To fix the issue, maintain profit center consistently in both WBS and network activity. If you want the WBS profit center to dominate, remove the profit center from the network so the system inherits from WBS. Alternatively, use OKB9 or a substitution rule to force profit center derivation during settlement. After correcting master data, re-run CJ88 and the error should disappear.
